name: Fish Emulsion
notes: Liquid organic nitrogen source. Fast-acting. Can smell unpleasant. Dilute before applying.

name: Bone Meal
notes: Slow-release phosphorus source. Promotes root development and flowering. Work into soil at planting.

name: Blood Meal
notes: Fast-acting organic nitrogen source. Acidifies soil slightly. Can burn plants if over-applied.

name: Kelp Meal
notes: Rich in trace minerals, growth hormones, and micronutrients. Improves soil structure and microbial activity.

name: Liquid Kelp Extract
notes: Concentrated seaweed extract. Promotes root growth, improves stress tolerance, and enhances nutrient uptake.

name: Worm Castings
notes: Gentle, balanced slow-release organic fertilizer with beneficial microbes. Cannot burn plants.

name: Compost Tea
notes: Brewed liquid compost. Inoculates soil with beneficial microorganisms. Apply as drench or foliar spray.

name: Balanced NPK 10-10-10
notes: Synthetic balanced fertilizer for general use. Good for establishing plants and overall growth.

name: Tomato & Vegetable Fertilizer 5-6-5
notes: Formulated for fruiting vegetables. Apply at transplant and every 4 weeks.

name: High-Nitrogen Feed 20-5-5
notes: For leafy greens and vigorous vegetative growth. Use cautiously on fruiting crops to avoid excess foliage.

name: Bloom Booster 2-10-10
notes: High phosphorus and potassium to encourage flowering and fruit set. Switch to this at first bud.

name: Garden Lime (Calcitic)
notes: Raises soil pH. Apply in autumn; takes several months to act. Add calcium. Test pH before applying.

name: Dolomitic Lime
notes: Raises pH while adding calcium and magnesium. Good for magnesium-deficient soils.

name: Sulfur Powder
notes: Lowers soil pH for acid-loving plants. Also a fungicide when dusted on foliage. Slow-acting.

name: Perlite
notes: Volcanic glass beads added to improve drainage and aeration in containers and heavy soils.

name: Vermiculite
notes: Expanded mineral that improves water retention and cation exchange capacity. Good in seed starting mixes.

name: Neem Oil
notes: Broad-spectrum organic pesticide/fungicide. Disrupts insect life cycle. Apply in early morning or evening. Not bee-safe when wet.

name: Diatomaceous Earth
notes: Fossilized algae; physically damages soft-bodied insects. Effective when dry. Reapply after rain.

name: Insecticidal Soap
notes: Kills soft-bodied insects on contact. Safe for humans and most beneficials when dry. Coat undersides of leaves.

name: Copper Fungicide
notes: Broad-spectrum fungicide and bactericide. Prevents blight, powdery mildew, and canker. Can accumulate in soil.

name: Sulfur Fungicide
notes: Preventive fungicide for powdery mildew and rust. Do not apply in temperatures above 30°C.

name: Bicarbonate Spray (Baking Soda)
notes: Raises surface pH to inhibit fungal spores. Best as a preventive treatment. Mix with horticultural oil to stick.

name: Epsom Salt (Magnesium Sulfate)
notes: Corrects magnesium and sulfur deficiency. Useful for tomatoes and peppers showing yellowing between leaf veins.

name: Wood Ash
notes: Raises soil pH and adds potassium and calcium. Do not use on acid-loving plants or near germinating seeds.

name: Mycorrhizal Inoculant
notes: Beneficial fungi that colonize roots, extending their effective reach. Apply directly to roots at transplant.
